text:
HEMATOLOGY/ONCOLOGY FOLLOW-UP NOTE
Department of Medical Oncology

ATTENDING PHYSICIAN: [Attending Oncologist]
FELLOW/RESIDENT: [Oncology Fellow]

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Mr. Okafor is a 58-year-old Nigerian-American male with metastatic sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma (stage IV, KRAS G12D-mutant, MSS) presenting for routine oncology follow-up. He is currently on second-line therapy with FOLFIRI plus ramucirumab and is here for assessment of response and toxicity following restaging imaging.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr. Okafor carries a diagnosis of stage IV KRAS G12D-mutant, microsatellite-stable adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon with synchronous bilobar hepatic metastases, now with pulmonary metastases identified at progression approximately 2.5 months ago. His disease history is summarized briefly below for context.

He was initially diagnosed approximately 13 months ago with a synchronous T3N2aM1a sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma. Molecular profiling at diagnosis was notable for KRAS G12D mutation, BRAF wild-type, MSS status, and a complex co-mutational landscape including TP53 R248W, APC loss, SMAD4 loss, PIK3CA E545K, and PTEN deletion. He completed 12 cycles of FOLFOX6 plus bevacizumab as first-line therapy with initial good response (partial response at cycle 6), though oxaliplatin was discontinued after cycle 8 due to dose-limiting grade 3 peripheral sensory neuropathy (cumulative oxaliplatin exposure approximately 510 mg/m2). Maintenance was continued with 5-FU/leucovorin plus bevacizumab. He underwent laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy approximately 7 months after diagnosis for local control of the primary tumor, with an uneventful postoperative course.

Approximately 10 months after diagnosis, restaging CT showed hepatic progression (enlargement of all four hepatic lesions, with the dominant segment VII lesion increasing from 1.8 to 2.2 cm) along with new bilateral pulmonary nodules (right lower lobe 8 mm, left upper lobe 6 mm, right middle lobe 5 mm). PET/CT confirmed FDG-avidity of all hepatic and pulmonary lesions, confirming progressive metastatic disease with M1b status (hepatic and pulmonary). CEA at progression was 62.3 ng/mL, up from nadir of 35.2 ng/mL. Anti-EGFR therapy remained contraindicated given KRAS G12D status; immunotherapy was not an option given MSS status.

Given progression on bevacizumab-containing maintenance therapy, second-line treatment was initiated with FOLFIRI plus ramucirumab (per RAISE trial data), irinotecan 180 mg/m2 day 1, leucovorin 400 mg/m2, 5-FU 400 mg/m2 bolus and 2400 mg/m2 over 46-hour infusion, and ramucirumab 8 mg/kg day 1, every 14 days. UGT1A1 genotyping prior to irinotecan showed *1/*28 heterozygous status (intermediate metabolizer), and irinotecan was started at standard dose with close monitoring. He developed grade 3 diarrhea after cycle 2 requiring IV hydration at the infusion center. Irinotecan was held for one cycle and subsequently dose-reduced to 150 mg/m2 (17% reduction). Ramucirumab-related hypertension also emerged, requiring intensification of his antihypertensive regimen -- amlodipine was uptitrated from 5 mg to 10 mg daily and hydrochlorothiazide 12.5 mg was added to his existing lisinopril 20 mg, achieving adequate blood pressure control.

He is now presenting after completing cycle 4 of FOLFIRI plus ramucirumab (at the reduced irinotecan dose), following restaging CT scan reviewed at today's visit, which shows a favorable response to second-line therapy.

Today, Mr. Okafor reports that he is feeling better than he was at the time of progression. His energy has improved. He denies any significant abdominal pain or cramping. He reports that diarrhea has resolved since the irinotecan dose reduction and with judicious loperamide use -- bowel movements are back to 1-2 per day, soft and formed. He notes mild nausea during the first 2-3 days of each cycle, well controlled with his antiemetic regimen (ondansetron, dexamethasone, and aprepitant). He reports mild fatigue, which he rates as manageable (grade 1-2), and he has been able to continue working part-time in an administrative capacity at the high school where he is principal. He notes grade 1-2 alopecia and is mildly self-conscious about hair thinning but tolerating this without significant distress. He reports no blood in stool, no abdominal distension, no obstruction symptoms, and no dyspnea at rest. He does still notice mild dyspnea on moderate exertion (walking briskly up stairs), though this has not worsened. He denies fever, chills, rigors, or signs of infection. He reports continued numbness and tingling in his feet bilaterally and the distal tips of his fingers from residual oxaliplatin-induced neuropathy; this has not worsened on the current irinotecan-based regimen as anticipated. He is taking gabapentin 300 mg three times daily for neuropathic discomfort. He denies falls but does continue to use a handrail when navigating stairs.

His blood pressure at home has been running in the 130s-140s systolic over the 80s-90s diastolic, which he considers improved. He has been adherent to his antihypertensive medications.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
- Constitutional: Fatigue grade 1-2. No unintentional weight loss. Appetite is fair but adequate. No fever, chills.
- HEENT: No mucositis or mouth sores this cycle. Mild alopecia.
- Cardiovascular: Blood pressure improved on current regimen. No chest pain. No palpitations. No lower extremity edema.
- Respiratory: Mild dyspnea on moderate exertion (stable, not worsening). No cough, hemoptysis.
- GI: Diarrhea resolved (previously grade 3, now grade 0). No nausea at rest; mild nausea days 1-3 of cycle, managed. No vomiting, abdominal pain, rectal bleeding, or changes at anastomosis site. Normal stool frequency (1-2/day).
- Neurological: Bilateral distal lower extremity numbness and tingling (grade 2, stable). Mild distal fingertip numbness (grade 1-2). No new neurological symptoms. No falls.
- Musculoskeletal: No joint pain or bone pain.
- Skin: Mild alopecia. No hand-foot syndrome. No rash.
- Genitourinary: No hematuria. Normal urine output. No proteinuria noted on last check.
- Psychiatric: Patient reports mild anxiety related to disease and treatment, but has good coping. Supportive of his family.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
(Previously documented; summarized)
1. Stage IV sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma (KRAS G12D, MSS, bilobar hepatic and pulmonary metastases) -- as above
2. Type 2 diabetes mellitus -- well-controlled on metformin; HbA1c has been 7.1-7.4% on recent checks
3. Hypertension -- previously on lisinopril alone, now on triple antihypertensive therapy (lisinopril 20 mg + amlodipine 10 mg + HCTZ 12.5 mg) due to bevacizumab/ramucirumab-related exacerbation
4. Hyperlipidemia -- on statin therapy
5. Oxaliplatin-induced peripheral sensory neuropathy (grade 2, residual; baseline diabetic neuropathy was grade 1 prior to chemotherapy)
6. Status post laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy with primary anastomosis (approximately 6 months prior)
7. Status post subcutaneous port placement (right chest wall)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
Mr. Okafor is a Nigerian-American male, age 58, who works as a high school principal (currently part-time, administrative duties only). He is a former smoker who quit approximately 12 years ago (15 pack-year history). He drinks alcohol only very rarely and stopped entirely since starting chemotherapy. He is married with three adult children and reports a strong family support network. He denies recreational drug use. He lives in a single-story home (modified to address fall risk given neuropathy).

FAMILY HISTORY:
No first-degree relatives with colorectal cancer. No known family history of Lynch syndrome or other hereditary cancer syndromes. Germline testing performed (per NGS reflex panel) was negative for pathogenic variants in APC, MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2, MUTYH, BRCA1, and BRCA2, consistent with sporadic colorectal cancer.

ALLERGIES:
No known drug allergies. Tolerating irinotecan (with dose reduction), ramucirumab, 5-FU, leucovorin without anaphylactic or hypersensitivity reactions.

MEDICATIONS:
1. 5-Fluorouracil 400 mg/m2 IV bolus day 1 + 2400 mg/m2 CIVI over 46 hours (every 14 days, via port)
2. Leucovorin 400 mg/m2 IV day 1 (every 14 days)
3. Irinotecan 150 mg/m2 IV day 1 (every 14 days; dose-reduced from 180 mg/m2 due to grade 3 diarrhea)
4. Ramucirumab 8 mg/kg IV day 1 (every 14 days)
5. Ondansetron 8 mg IV day 1 (pre-chemotherapy), then 8 mg PO TID PRN nausea x 3 days
6. Dexamethasone 8 mg IV day 1 (pre-chemotherapy)
7. Aprepitant 125 mg PO day 1, 80 mg PO days 2-3
8. Loperamide 4 mg PO at first loose stool, then 2 mg every 2 hours as needed (for diarrhea)
9. Atropine 0.25 mg SQ available for cholinergic symptoms (early diarrhea/cramping during infusion)
10. Lisinopril 20 mg PO daily
11. Amlodipine 10 mg PO daily
12. Hydrochlorothiazide 12.5 mg PO daily
13. Gabapentin 300 mg PO TID (for neuropathic discomfort)
14. Metformin 1000 mg PO BID
15. Atorvastatin 40 mg PO nightly
16. Aspirin 81 mg PO daily

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vital Signs: BP 138/88 mmHg (right arm, seated), HR 72 bpm, RR 16, SpO2 97% on room air, weight 86.2 kg (BMI 28.6), Temperature 37.0 C.

General: Mr. Okafor is a well-appearing, well-nourished male in no acute distress. He is alert and oriented x3, conversant and pleasant. Mild scalp alopecia noted.

HEENT: Atraumatic, normocephalic. Oropharynx moist, no mucositis or oral ulcers. Mild alopecia of the scalp. No lymphadenopathy in the cervical or supraclavicular chains.

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs. No jugular venous distention. No peripheral edema in bilateral lower extremities.

Respiratory: Lungs clear to auscultation bilaterally. No crackles or wheezes. No dullness to percussion. Breathing unlabored at rest.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Healing laparoscopic incision sites from prior sigmoid colectomy are well-healed and non-tender. No palpable hepatomegaly or splenomegaly. No palpable abdominal or pelvic masses. Bowel sounds normal.

Neurological: Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Mild decreased vibratory sensation in bilateral feet and distal lower extremities (stocking distribution, grade 2). Mild decreased sensation to light touch in bilateral fingertips (glove distribution, grade 1-2). Proprioception mildly impaired at the toes bilaterally. Deep tendon reflexes: 1+ at Achilles bilaterally (diminished but present), 2+ at patellae bilaterally. Gait slightly cautious but stable with normal stride length. No ataxia on tandem gait. Romberg mildly positive.

Port Site: Right chest wall port site is clean, dry, and intact. No erythema, induration, or signs of infection. Port accessible without difficulty.

Skin: Mild alopecia. No hand-foot syndrome. No rash. No pallor, jaundice, or cyanosis.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS:
Complete Blood Count:
- WBC: 4.8 x10^9/L (within normal limits; no significant leukopenia)
- ANC: 2.4 x10^9/L (adequate)
- Hemoglobin: 11.4 g/dL (mild anemia, stable; consistent with chemotherapy-related and disease-related anemia)
- Platelets: 214 x10^9/L (normal)

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
- Sodium: 138 mEq/L, Potassium: 3.9 mEq/L, CO2: 24 mEq/L, BUN: 16 mg/dL, Creatinine: 1.02 mg/dL (eGFR >60)
- Total bilirubin: 0.8 mg/dL, AST: 28 U/L, ALT: 32 U/L, ALP: 98 U/L (all within normal limits; no hepatic dysfunction)
- Albumin: 3.8 g/dL
- Glucose: 118 mg/dL (mildly elevated, consistent with post-prandial and steroid exposure)

Tumor Markers:
- CEA: 41.2 ng/mL (down from 62.3 ng/mL at the time of progression 2.5 months ago; approaching nadir of 35.2 ng/mL achieved during first-line maintenance)
- CA 19-9: 102 U/mL (down from 148 U/mL at progression)

Urinalysis: No proteinuria (negative on dipstick; 1+ protein on prior check has resolved with blood pressure optimization -- reassuring in the context of ramucirumab therapy). No hematuria.

HbA1c (checked last month): 7.3% -- acceptable glycemic control.

---

IMAGING RESULTS:
Restaging CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis with IV contrast (reviewed at today's visit, obtained after cycle 4 of FOLFIRI plus ramucirumab):

The CT shows a favorable response to second-line therapy. The hepatic lesions have all either decreased or remained stable. The dominant segment VII lesion has come down from 2.2 cm at the time of progression to 2.0 cm now. The segment IVa lesion has decreased slightly from 1.3 to 1.2 cm. Segment II and segment VI lesions are essentially unchanged at 1.0 cm each. The three pulmonary nodules also appear to have responded: the right lower lobe nodule is down from 8 to 7 mm, the left upper lobe nodule from 6 to 5 mm, and the right middle lobe 5 mm nodule is stable. The anastomotic site from the prior sigmoid colectomy is unremarkable without recurrence. No new hepatic or pulmonary nodules. No ascites. No peritoneal deposits. Taking all target lesions together, the tumor burden has decreased from 5.5 cm at the time of progression to approximately 4.2 cm now -- a roughly 24% reduction from progression baseline -- consistent with a partial response to second-line therapy.

CEA trajectory mirrors the imaging findings, with a decline from 62.3 to 41.2 ng/mL. CA 19-9 similarly down from 148 to 102 U/mL.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

Mr. Okafor is a 58-year-old man with stage IV KRAS G12D-mutant, MSS sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma with hepatic and pulmonary metastases, now on second-line FOLFIRI plus ramucirumab. He is demonstrating a partial response to second-line therapy by both imaging and tumor marker criteria, and his performance status remains excellent at ECOG PS 1.

1. METASTATIC COLORECTAL CANCER -- PARTIAL RESPONSE ON SECOND-LINE FOLFIRI PLUS RAMUCIRUMAB:
   Imaging is encouraging. Hepatic and pulmonary lesions have all either decreased or stabilized, and tumor markers (CEA down to 41.2, CA 19-9 down to 102) are tracking in the right direction. We will continue FOLFIRI plus ramucirumab at current doses (irinotecan 150 mg/m2 after prior dose reduction). I have counseled Mr. Okafor that this response is meaningful and that the goal is to maintain it as long as possible. I have also discussed the importance of early reporting of diarrhea given his prior grade 3 episode -- strict loperamide protocol reinforced. Next restaging CT planned in approximately 8 weeks (after 4 additional cycles).

   Longer-term considerations: given the recently identified acquired HER2 amplification on repeat biopsy (ERBB2 copy number 8, also detected in ctDNA), HER2-directed therapy will be considered at next progression. Options will include trastuzumab plus pertuzumab as HER2-directed doublet (supported by MyPathway and similar data), or trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) given emerging data in HER2-amplified solid tumors including colorectal. Mr. Okafor has been referred to the early-phase clinical trials team, and pre-screening for a phase II trial investigating a KRAS G12D-specific inhibitor combination is underway. I emphasized to him that remaining in good performance status and maintaining adequate organ function is critical to maximizing his clinical trial eligibility.

2. PERIPHERAL SENSORY NEUROPATHY (OXALIPLATIN-INDUCED, GRADE 2, RESIDUAL):
   Neuropathy is stable at grade 2, not worsening on the current irinotecan-based regimen as expected (irinotecan does not cause cumulative sensory neuropathy). He has adapted well with occupational therapy guidance and assistive devices. Gabapentin 300 mg TID is providing modest symptom relief. I discussed duloxetine as an additional option for neuropathic pain -- patient will consider. Continuing neurology follow-up. I reminded him that grade 2 neuropathy may persist given the cumulative oxaliplatin burden but could improve slowly over 12-18 months. Fall precautions reinforced.

3. TREATMENT-RELATED HYPERTENSION (BEVACIZUMAB/RAMUCIRUMAB-EXACERBATED):
   Blood pressure today 138/88 mmHg -- improved on triple antihypertensive therapy (lisinopril 20 mg + amlodipine 10 mg + HCTZ 12.5 mg). No proteinuria on today's urinalysis, which is reassuring. Will continue current antihypertensive regimen. Will recheck BP at next infusion visit. If systolic exceeds 160 or proteinuria develops (>1 g/24h), we would need to consider holding or dose-modifying ramucirumab per protocol.

4. ANEMIA:
   Hemoglobin 11.4 g/dL, mild, stable. Patient is asymptomatic from this. No transfusion at this time. Iron studies to be checked at next visit to rule out iron deficiency component. No erythropoiesis-stimulating agents given metastatic disease setting.

5. TYPE 2 DIABETES MELLITUS:
   HbA1c 7.3% -- acceptable. Continuing metformin 1000 mg BID. Glucose mildly elevated today, likely post-prandial and related to dexamethasone use on infusion days. No insulin initiation at this time. Will watch.

6. PSYCHOSOCIAL:
   Patient remains highly motivated and engaged in his care. He is working part-time and maintaining a meaningful quality of life. I acknowledged his anxiety about disease progression and reassured him that we have meaningful additional options -- including HER2-directed therapy and potential clinical trial eligibility -- at future lines. Encouraged him to continue engaging with his support network. Social work and palliative care support available if needed; patient deferred at this time, though he is aware of the option.

7. FOLLOW-UP:
   - Return to clinic in 2 weeks for cycle 5 of FOLFIRI plus ramucirumab.
   - CBC and CMP at each cycle.
   - Tumor markers (CEA, CA 19-9) every 4 weeks.
   - Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis in approximately 8 weeks (after cycle 8).
   - Iron studies at next visit.
   - Neurology follow-up (neuropathy management) -- pending scheduling.
   - Clinical trials pre-screening ongoing (KRAS G12D-targeted therapy trial).
   - Blood pressure monitoring at each infusion visit.

Overall, Mr. Okafor is doing well given the complexity of his disease. He continues to demonstrate good performance status and resilience. The partial response to FOLFIRI plus ramucirumab is gratifying, and the goal is to maintain disease control as long as possible while preserving quality of life and positioning him for subsequent lines of therapy.

Time spent with patient: 45 minutes (greater than 50% of visit time spent in counseling and coordination of care).

[Electronically signed by Attending Oncologist]
